title: Crash recovery integration test corpus + failure-mode coverage matrix
goal: Build the end-to-end crash recovery test corpus that exercises every documented failure mode of cluster X.3 in an integration setting: clean shutdown, SIGKILL mid-iteration, Postgres connection loss mid-checkpoint, orphan processes, event-seq gaps, idempotency conflicts, and operator-cancel during recovery. Mandatory because the folded WP-1-Session-Crash-Recovery-Checkpointing-v1 stub demands 'deterministic failure modes and explicit evidence.'
expected_diff_shape: New integration test crate src/backend/handshake_core/tests/crash_recovery_e2e/ with one test file per failure mode + a shared harness module + a goldenfile evidence corpus under tests/fixtures/crash_recovery_evidence/.
unblock_conditions: All X.3 primitives + restart orchestrator + idempotency layer merged.
implementation_notes: Each test file exercises one failure mode with a deterministic seed and expected ResumeReport. Shared harness module provides: (a) `CrashRecoveryHarness::new(scenario)` spins up a Postgres test container, seeds it with a scenario fixture, then runs a Handshake-subprocess (or in-process simulator) until the simulated crash point; (b) `harness.simulate_crash(kind)` executes the failure (SIGKILL, db disconnect, etc.); (c) `harness.restart()` invokes RestartResumeOrchestrator (MT-193) on the fresh process; (d) `harness.expect_resume_report(expected: ResumeReport)` asserts the report matches and persists evidence to tests/fixtures/crash_recovery_evidence/<scenario>.json for forensic comparison. Failure mode matrix: clean_shutdown -> all sessions resumed cleanly; sigkill_mid_iteration -> sessions resumed with one outcome=Failure recorded for the killed MT; postgres_loss -> orchestrator emits FR-EVT-RESTART-RESUME-DB-UNAVAILABLE, retries with backoff, eventually resumes when db returns; orphan_process -> reclaim hook (MT-186 forced cancellation) invoked, then resume; event_seq_gap -> sessions_recovery_failed populated, operator decision_request posted; idempotency_conflict -> AlreadyApplied path verified, no double side effects; operator_cancel_during_recovery -> resume aborted gracefully, session marked Cancelled with reason. Every test asserts: (1) ResumeReport.sessions_resumed and sessions_recovery_failed counts exact; (2) FR events emitted in expected order; (3) goldenfile match (tolerant of timestamps via canonicalization). The whole suite runs under `cargo test -p handshake_core --test crash_recovery_e2e`.
risk_if_missed: Without an integration test corpus, all X.3 unit tests pass individually but the end-to-end recovery path may silently break — and the operator only finds out during a real crash. The folded stub's 'deterministic failure modes and explicit evidence' invariant cannot be validated.
validator_focus: Every failure mode has a dedicated test; goldenfile evidence corpus is byte-stable (modulo canonicalized timestamps); FR event ordering asserted not just presence.

implementation_record.summary: MT-195 now combines the deterministic golden crash-recovery corpus with live Postgres runtime-chaos evidence. The golden suite still covers clean shutdown, SIGKILL-style interruption, Postgres loss, orphan process reclaim, event-seq gap, idempotency conflict, and operator cancel. The live runtime-chaos tests additionally prove persisted kernel_restart_resume_report rows, role mailbox decision rows, checkpoint/process evidence after a hard-killed test child process, pg_terminate_backend database-loss evidence without shared database damage, a real handshake_core app-runtime binary startup-recovery pass against production migrations/tables in an isolated Postgres schema, and transient DB-unavailable backoff/reconnect resume without stopping or damaging shared Postgres.

red_team_coverage.golden_canonicalization: The harness drops volatile UUIDs/timestamps/paths and compares stable exact-count evidence fields plus FR event order to per-scenario JSON golden files.
red_team_coverage.exact_counts: Every golden scenario asserts exact sessions_examined, sessions_resumed, sessions_recovery_failed, operator decision request, orphan reclaim, idempotency, and cancellation counts before golden comparison.
red_team_coverage.serial_execution: All proof commands run with --test-threads=1; live Postgres tests isolate schemas and clean up after themselves.
red_team_coverage.live_persisted_reports: runtime_chaos::mt195_runtime_* tests assert live kernel_restart_resume_report rows for hard-kill, backend termination, event-gap, and operator-cancel scenarios.
red_team_coverage.live_mailbox_decision_rows: event-gap and operator-cancel runtime-chaos tests assert persisted role_mailbox_v1 decision evidence rather than only in-memory hook calls.
red_team_coverage.backend_termination: runtime_chaos::mt195_runtime_backend_termination_records_db_loss_without_shared_db_damage uses pg_terminate_backend to force a database connection loss and verifies report evidence without damaging the shared local database.
red_team_coverage.hard_kill_child_process: runtime_chaos::mt195_runtime_hard_kill_child_leaves_checkpoint_process_evidence_and_persists_report spawns and kills the test-binary child entrypoint, then verifies checkpoint/process/report evidence.
red_team_coverage.real_handshake_core_binary_startup_recov: runtime_chaos::mt195_runtime_real_handshake_core_binary_runs_startup_recovery_and_persists_report spawns the real handshake_core app-runtime binary in quiet startup-recovery-only mode, points it at an isolated production-migrated Postgres schema, and verifies report, queue, post-failure checkpoint, orphan reclaim, and final counter evidence.
red_team_coverage.transient_db_unavailable_backoff: runtime_chaos::mt195_runtime_transient_db_unavailable_backs_off_and_resumes_after_db_return_without_shared_db_damage closes the first PgPool to force a transient DB-unavailable attempt, observes bounded backoff, reconnects to the same isolated production schema, resumes recovery, persists the DB-unavailable FR event in the report, and avoids stopping shared Postgres.

residual_risks: Hard-kill runtime evidence still kills a test-binary child entrypoint; the new real handshake_core app-runtime proof covers production startup restart-resume and production tables, but it does not kill a real production model-run subprocess mid-iteration.

DB-loss runtime evidence now combines pg_terminate_backend connection-loss proof with closed-pool transient backoff/reconnect proof in an isolated production schema; it still does not stop and restart the PostgreSQL server or container itself.
Operator-cancel runtime evidence proves persisted report/mailbox decision behavior through the runtime chaos harness, not through a real operator UI workflow.

out_of_scope_self_evaluation.why: MT-195 stayed primarily in test/fixture surfaces, but the live runtime-chaos extension depends on MT-193 production restart-resume persistence and role-mailbox rows so the evidence can validate real database artifacts instead of only golden in-memory reports.

minimum_controls: Goldenfile canonicalization documented in harness (timestamps to T-N notation, UUIDs to U-N notation, paths to repo-relative).
Each failure mode test asserts EXACT counts not just non-zero (e.g., sessions_resumed == 3 not >= 1).
Integration suite runs serially (--test-threads=1) because shared Postgres test container is the bottleneck; documented in test module preamble.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Golden crash-recovery corpus covers all 7 documented failure modes with byte-stable canonicalized JSON evidence; live runtime-chaos slice adds 6 live Postgres tests including a real handshake_core app-runtime binary startup-recovery proof, pg_terminate_backend DB-loss proof, hard-kill child-process proof, and transient-DB-unavailable backoff proof.
